source: RMC-BestFit/src/RMC.BestFit/Estimation/MaximumLikelihood.cs @ fc28c0c ; core/tests/test_maximum_likelihood.cpp -- exact oracle values dumped from the REAL C# MaximumLikelihood via tools/oracle_emitter (Task T12). NelderMead is a deterministic simplex (no RNG, no parallel reduction), so the optimized quantities (parameter/max_log_likelihood/aic/bic) reproduce to rel 1e-9 across the 4 independently-built cores. Diagonal covariance/standard_error/correlation are one numerical-Hessian finite-difference step more rounding-sensitive (rel 1e-5, per fixtures/README.md's tolerance policy). The off-diagonal covariance/correlation are theoretically zero for a Normal MLE (mu and sigma are orthogonal); the tiny nonzero values are finite-difference residuals, checked with an ABSOLUTE tolerance (rel would divide by ~0).
